# sleekblue

A [pkgdown](https://pkgdown.r-lib.org) **template package** that gives
your documentation site a sleek, muted-blue look with first-class
**light and dark colour modes**. It bundles a coordinated type scale, a
light/dark syntax-highlighting pair, and component styling.

## What you get

- **Muted-blue palette** — a calm steel-blue (`#3f6f9f`) primary on a
  faintly cool off-white in light mode, and a brighter airy blue
  (`#7ea8d6`) on deep muted navy in dark mode.
- **Light + dark modes** via pkgdown’s light switch (Bootstrap 5.3
  colour modes). A toggle appears in the navbar; code highlighting swaps
  between `arrow-light` and `nord` to match.
- **Typography** — Inter for body text, Outfit for headings, JetBrains
  Mono for code (bundled at build time, no CDN needed at view time).
- **Polished components** — rounded, framed code blocks; accent-bar TOC
  links; a subtle blurred navbar border; tidy tables, buttons, and focus
  rings.

## Usage

In the consuming package’s `_pkgdown.yml`:

``` yaml
template:
  package: sleekblue
```

For pkgdown to find the template when building the site (e.g. on CI),
add it to the `DESCRIPTION`:

    Config/Needs/website: theabhirath/sleekblue

### Installation

``` r

# install.packages("pak")
pak::pak("theabhirath/sleekblue")
```

## Customising

- Colours, fonts, and Bootstrap variables live in
  `inst/pkgdown/_pkgdown.yml` under `template.bslib`.
- Layout and component styling — including the entire dark colour mode
  under `[data-bs-theme="dark"]` — lives in `inst/pkgdown/extra.scss`.

A consuming package can override any single value by setting it locally
in its own `_pkgdown.yml`; local keys win over the template’s.

# 

# Articles

### All vignettes

- [Colours &
  modes](https://theabhirath.github.io/sleekblue/articles/colours-and-modes.md):

  The muted-blue palette and how the light and dark colour modes are
  wired up.

- [Theme
  showcase](https://theabhirath.github.io/sleekblue/articles/showcase.md):

  A kitchen-sink of common documentation elements so you can see how the
  muted-blue theme styles headings, prose, code, tables, and figures —
  in both light and dark mode.
